reasoning: Both audits confirm the PKGBUILD only downloads/installs static codecs from official source with checksum verification. No build/install hooks or dangerous operations exist. Skipped binary is expected for codec packages.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped auditing essential-amd64-20071007.tar.bz2 (binary codec payload)
No review of actual binary contents despite being primary payload
false_negatives: HTTP download without HTTPS (mitigated by checksum but still a hygiene risk)
Potential license compliance issues with proprietary codecs (not security-critical)
learnings: Improve binary handling: Extract archives to audit contained scripts/executables
Prioritize files referenced in PKGBUILD functions over documentation like README
Add HTTP/HTTPS transport assessment to network checks
Develop standard patterns for safe binary blob packages
